script:
Before: I couldn't sit in a quiet room. The ringing was so constant and so loud that
silence had become my enemy. I slept with three fans and a podcast playing.

A year ago my tinnitus was a 7 out of 10 on bad days. It affected my concentration, my
sleep, my mood. I was short with people I loved because I was exhausted from the
noise inside my own head.

Before: My ENT told me there was nothing that could be done. I should get a hearing
aid to mask it. Learn to live with it.
I tried to accept that. For two years I tried.

Before: I couldn't sit in a quiet room. The ringing was so constant and so loud that
silence had become my enemy. I slept with three fans and a podcast playing.

Six months ago I was researching whether the ringing would drive me to anxiety
medication. It had gotten that bad
